page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d family. It features 16 dedicated virtual central processing units (vCPUs) and 126.0 gigabytes (GB) of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of approximately 7.88 GB per vCPU, positioning the instance for memory-intensive workloads that require dedicated compute resources without shared-core overhead.

This server configuration does not include local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance features zero graphics processing units (GPUs) and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These hardware omissions represent clear architectural tradeoffs, prioritizing memory capacity and dedicated compute over local storage speed and direct external network addressing.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per vCPU allows for efficient resource utilization in workloads that are memory-bound rather than compute-bound. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution times. This profile is appropriate for workloads such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 126.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.88 GB per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-intensive applications. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance for workloads. This server is designed for memory-bound tasks, including database hosting, in-memory caching, and data processing pipelines that require significant RAM capacity relative to compute resources.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 126 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPUs, making it a dedicated option for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-16 features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 126 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server configuration offering high memory density with 126 GB RAM and 16 vCPUs for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 126.0 GB of system memory providing 7.88 GB per core
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Designed for memory-bound workloads and database hosting
